intended_use: Saige-Dx analyzes digital breast tomosynthesis (DBT) mammograms to identify the presence or absence of soft tissue lesions and calcifications that may be indicative of cancer. For a given DBT mammogra
intended_use_full: Saige-Dx analyzes digital breast tomosynthesis (DBT) mammograms to identify the presence or absence of soft tissue lesions and calcifications that may be indicative of cancer. For a given DBT mammogram, Saige-Dx analyzes the DBT image stacks and the accompanying 2D images, including full field digital mammography and/or synthetic images. The system assigns a Suspicion Level, indicating the strength of suspicion that cancer may be present, for each detected finding and for the entire case. The outputs of Saige-Dx are intended to be used as a concurrent reading aid for interpreting physicians on screening mammograms with compatible DBT hardware.
target_condition: breast cancer screening; detection of soft tissue lesions and calcifications indicative of cancer
ground_truth: MQSA qualified breast imaging specialists serving as truthers with independent review of cancer exams and supporting medical reports by two independent truthers plus an additional adjudicator if needed; cancer confirmed by biopsy pathology; non-cancer confirmed by negative interpretation on subsequent screen
other_metrics: Case-level AUC on compatible exams: 0.910 (95% CI: 0.886-0.933); performance was non-inferior to predicate device; met pre-specified performance criteria on Hologic HD images, unilateral breasts, and breast implants with implant displaced views
